The Higher Education Commission (HEC) has finalized the project to revamp its degree attestation system by integrating blockchain technology, aiming to enhance efficiency and transparency while eliminating the need for applicants’ physical presence.

Depends on the job, but if they require a degree and you claim to have one, the background screening will connect with the university’s database and verify. Source: I worked for a background screener for nearly 4 years and did this all the time

As someone in HR who reviews the background check, I promise if it says required we’re checking for it. I had a doctor, licensed in NY, and I still had to reach out to his German university to verify his degree. If the company doesn’t hold the same standard for all roles, run.

Fully on-chain art isn’t just a technical choice. It’s a cultural one.

Most NFTs you see aren’t actually “forever.” They’re off-chain images often stored on IPFS. Sounds decentralized, but there’s a problem: IPFS isn’t free. Someone has to pay the pinning costs. If the platform

IPFS deserves credit for moving decentralized storage forward, but the gaps are hard to ignore: no incentives for permanence, unpredictable retrieval times, and weak discovery. 

That’s why so many teams end up relying on pinning services that recreate the same centralization

Most Web3 builders use IPFS.

But here’s the problem,

 Files can disappear

 Pinning costs pile up

 Data is not guaranteed to stay online

With Irys you can solve all of these.

You upload once.
The data stays on Arweave permanently.
No extra fees and no missing content.

3/ Problem #1: Disappearing Artwork 
• IPFS requires perpetual payment for pinning 
• Marketplaces shut down, taking your art with them 
• @Nahiiko's "Bit Rot" collection brilliantly demonstrates this: https://opensea.io/collection/bitrot-by-nahiko…

Affordable on chain storage is a game changer. Today, Web3 projects often hit a wall with storage costs. Whether you’re pinning data to IPFS or paying heavy Arweave fees, unpredictable costs make scaling hard. @Irys_xyz tackles this head on with a simple promise: low, stable

DAY 2 of saying Hirys until @irys_xyz Mainnet

Unlike centralized storage solutions that rely on mutable servers, Irys stores data directly on its Layer-1 blockchain. 

This ensures that data is not subject to deletion, downtime, or third-party control.

On-chain storage

Irys (Blockchain Datachain)

@irys_xyz isn’t just storing data like Filecoin or Arweave, they’ve made data itself programmable.

This means AI agents and smart contracts can read, update, and act on data directly on-chain, at very high speed and low cost.

It’s storage + compute
